questions.question: Which component of a computer is considered the brain of the system?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) performs calculations and controls all major operations of a computer.

questions.question: Which type of memory is volatile in nature?
questions.explanation: RAM is volatile memory, meaning its data is lost when the power supply is turned off.

questions.question: Which file extension is commonly used for Microsoft Word documents?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Word files are typically saved with the .docx extension.

questions.question: Which device is used to print documents on paper?
questions.explanation: A printer produces a hard copy of digital documents on paper.

questions.question: What does URL stand for?
questions.explanation: URL stands for Uniform Resource Locator, which is the address of a webpage on the internet.

questions.question: Which key combination is commonly used to undo the last action?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + Z is used to undo the most recent action in most applications.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an operating system?
questions.explanation: Windows is an operating system that manages hardware and software resources of a computer.

questions.question: What is the main function of an antivirus program?
questions.explanation: Antivirus software detects and removes viruses and other malicious programs.

questions.question: Which storage device is commonly used for portable data transfer?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is used to transfer and store data conveniently.

questions.question: Which protocol is primarily used to transfer web pages on the internet?
questions.explanation: HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol) is used for transferring web pages over the internet.
